# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven customer help chatbot using C# / Blazor that answers questions from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for all data storage and a local `Docs/` folder as the document source.

## Technology Selection (per decision tree)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI | **Blazor Server** (Interactive Server rendering) | Real-time streaming, no WASM download, C# end-to-end |
| LLM abstraction | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with context; no tool calling needed, so no Agent Framework required |
| LLM provider | **OpenAI** NuGet package | Concrete provider behind MEAI; user can swap to Azure.AI.OpenAI later |
| Vector store | **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **Npgsql pgvector connector** | Matches Postgres requirement; provider-agnostic vector search |
| PDF text extraction | **UglyToad.PdfPig** | Free, MIT-licensed, pure-C# PDF parser |
| Relational ORM | **EF Core + Npgsql** | Chat history, session management in the same Postgres instance |
| Embedding model | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` via MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Cost-effective, good quality |
| Chat model | OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Low cost, sufficient quality for doc Q&A |

## Architecture Overview

```
┌──────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐       ┌──────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │◄──────►│  ChatService     │──────►│ Postgres │
│  (streaming) │        │  (IChatClient)   │       │ (EF Core │
└──────────────┘        │                  │       │ +pgvector)│
                        │  Embed query ──► │       └──────────┘
                        │  Vector search ► │
                        │  Build prompt  ► │
                        │  Stream answer ► │
                        └──────────────────┘

┌───────────────────┐
│ Ingestion Service │ ── scan Docs/ ──► extract text ──► chunk ──► embed ──► upsert pgvector
└───────────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Convert to Blazor Server project (add Razor components, static assets)
- Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.Extensions.AI, OpenAI, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions, Npgsql pgvector connector, Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL, UglyToad.PdfPig
- Configure DI: IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, vector store, EF DbContext
- Add appsettings.json sections (connection string, AI options — no secrets)

### 2. Data model & EF Core setup
- `ChatSession` entity (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` record for pgvector (Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding)
- EF migrations for Postgres + pgvector extension

### 3. PDF ingestion pipeline
- `IngestionService` — scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text page-by-page with PdfPig
- Chunk by paragraphs/sections (~500 tokens, ~100 token overlap)
- Generate embeddings via IEmbeddingGenerator (batched)
- Upsert chunks into pgvector via VectorData abstractions
- Expose as a background hosted service that runs on startup

### 4. RAG chat service
- `ChatService` receives user question + session ID
- Embed the question
- Vector search (top-5, min score 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks + source attribution
- Call IChatClient with streaming (IAsyncEnumerable)
- Persist user message + assistant response to ChatMessage table
- Guardrails: temperature 0, max tokens, retry logic

### 5. Blazor UI
- Layout with sidebar (session list) + main chat area
- Chat page: message history, input box, streaming response display
- "New Chat" button creates a ChatSession
- Basic CSS styling (clean, professional)
- Loading indicators during LLM streaming

### 6. Configuration & guardrails
- Secrets via user-secrets (OpenAI API key)
- IOptions<AiOptions> for model names, temperature, max tokens
- Health check for OpenAI connectivity
- Structured logging of token usage (no PII)

## Notes
- The user will need a running Postgres instance with the `vector` extension enabled (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`)
- API key configured via `dotnet user-secrets set "AI:ApiKey" "<key>"`
- The `Docs/` folder is watched at startup; drop PDFs in and restart (or call an ingestion endpoint) to re-index
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a straightforward RAG pattern (retrieve context → single LLM call)

| UI | **Blazor Server** (Interactive Server rendering) | Real-time streaming, no WASM download, C# end-to-end |
| LLM abstraction | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with context; no tool calling needed, so no Agent Framework required |
| LLM provider | **OpenAI** NuGet package | Concrete provider behind MEAI; user can swap to Azure.AI.OpenAI later |
| Vector store | **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **Npgsql pgvector connector** | Matches Postgres requirement; provider-agnostic vector search |
| PDF text extraction | **UglyToad.PdfPig** | Free, MIT-licensed, pure-C# PDF parser |
| Relational ORM | **EF Core + Npgsql** | Chat history, session management in the same Postgres instance |
| Embedding model | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` via MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Cost-effective, good quality |
| Chat model | OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Low cost, sufficient quality for doc Q&A |

## Architecture Overview

```
┌──────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐       ┌──────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │◄──────►│  ChatService     │──────►│ Postgres │
│  (streaming) │        │  (IChatClient)   │       │ (EF Core │
└──────────────┘        │                  │       │ +pgvector)│
                        │  Embed query ──► │       └──────────┘
                        │  Vector search ► │
                        │  Build prompt  ► │
                        │  Stream answer ► │
                        └──────────────────┘

┌───────────────────┐
│ Ingestion Service │ ── scan Docs/ ──► extract text ──► chunk ──► embed ──► upsert pgvector
└───────────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Convert to Blazor Server project (add Razor components, static assets)
- Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.Extensions.AI, OpenAI, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions, Npgsql pgvector connector, Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL, UglyToad.PdfPig
- Configure DI: IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, vector store, EF DbContext
- Add appsettings.json sections (connection string, AI options — no secrets)

### 2. Data model & EF Core setup
- `ChatSession` entity (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` record for pgvector (Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding)
- EF migrations for Postgres + pgvector extension

### 3. PDF ingestion pipeline
- `IngestionService` — scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text page-by-page with PdfPig
- Chunk by paragraphs/sections (~500 tokens, ~100 token overlap)
- Generate embeddings via IEmbeddingGenerator (batched)
- Upsert chunks into pgvector via VectorData abstractions
- Expose as a background hosted service that runs on startup

### 4. RAG chat service
- `ChatService` receives user question + session ID
- Embed the question
- Vector search (top-5, min score 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks + source attribution
- Call IChatClient with streaming (IAsyncEnumerable)
- Persist user message + assistant response to ChatMessage table
- Guardrails: temperature 0, max tokens, retry logic

### 5. Blazor UI
- Layout with sidebar (session list) + main chat area
- Chat page: message history, input box, streaming response display
- "New Chat" button creates a ChatSession
- Basic CSS styling (clean, professional)
- Loading indicators during LLM streaming

### 6. Configuration & guardrails
- Secrets via user-secrets (OpenAI API key)
- IOptions<AiOptions> for model names, temperature, max tokens
- Health check for OpenAI connectivity
- Structured logging of token usage (no PII)

## Notes
- The user will need a running Postgres instance with the `vector` extension enabled (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`)
- API key configured via `dotnet user-secrets set "AI:ApiKey" "<key>"`
- The `Docs/` folder is watched at startup; drop PDFs in and restart (or call an ingestion endpoint) to re-index
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a straightforward RAG pattern (retrieve context → single LLM call)

| UI | **Blazor Server** (Interactive Server rendering) | Real-time streaming, no WASM download, C# end-to-end |
| LLM abstraction | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with context; no tool calling needed, so no Agent Framework required |
| LLM provider | **OpenAI** NuGet package | Concrete provider behind MEAI; user can swap to Azure.AI.OpenAI later |
| Vector store | **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **Npgsql pgvector connector** | Matches Postgres requirement; provider-agnostic vector search |
| PDF text extraction | **UglyToad.PdfPig** | Free, MIT-licensed, pure-C# PDF parser |
| Relational ORM | **EF Core + Npgsql** | Chat history, session management in the same Postgres instance |
| Embedding model | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` via MEAI `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Cost-effective, good quality |
| Chat model | OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Low cost, sufficient quality for doc Q&A |

## Architecture Overview

```
┌──────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐       ┌──────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │◄──────►│  ChatService     │──────►│ Postgres │
│  (streaming) │        │  (IChatClient)   │       │ (EF Core │
└──────────────┘        │                  │       │ +pgvector)│
                        │  Embed query ──► │       └──────────┘
                        │  Vector search ► │
                        │  Build prompt  ► │
                        │  Stream answer ► │
                        └──────────────────┘

┌───────────────────┐
│ Ingestion Service │ ── scan Docs/ ──► extract text ──► chunk ──► embed ──► upsert pgvector
└───────────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Convert to Blazor Server project (add Razor components, static assets)
- Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.Extensions.AI, OpenAI, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions, Npgsql pgvector connector, Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL, UglyToad.PdfPig
- Configure DI: IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, vector store, EF DbContext
- Add appsettings.json sections (connection string, AI options — no secrets)

### 2. Data model & EF Core setup
- `ChatSession` entity (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` record for pgvector (Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding)
- EF migrations for Postgres + pgvector extension

### 3. PDF ingestion pipeline
- `IngestionService` — scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text page-by-page with PdfPig
- Chunk by paragraphs/sections (~500 tokens, ~100 token overlap)
- Generate embeddings via IEmbeddingGenerator (batched)
- Upsert chunks into pgvector via VectorData abstractions
- Expose as a background hosted service that runs on startup

### 4. RAG chat service
- `ChatService` receives user question + session ID
- Embed the question
- Vector search (top-5, min score 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks + source attribution
- Call IChatClient with streaming (IAsyncEnumerable)
- Persist user message + assistant response to ChatMessage table
- Guardrails: temperature 0, max tokens, retry logic

### 5. Blazor UI
- Layout with sidebar (session list) + main chat area
- Chat page: message history, input box, streaming response display
- "New Chat" button creates a ChatSession
- Basic CSS styling (clean, professional)
- Loading indicators during LLM streaming

### 6. Configuration & guardrails
- Secrets via user-secrets (OpenAI API key)
- IOptions<AiOptions> for model names, temperature, max tokens
- Health check for OpenAI connectivity
- Structured logging of token usage (no PII)

## Notes
- The user will need a running Postgres instance with the `vector` extension enabled (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`)
- API key configured via `dotnet user-secrets set "AI:ApiKey" "<key>"`
- The `Docs/` folder is watched at startup; drop PDFs in and restart (or call an ingestion endpoint) to re-index
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a straightforward RAG pattern (retrieve context → single LLM call)

Here's the plan:

## HelpChatbot — Plan Summary

**Approach:** A RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) chatbot — ingest PDFs → chunk → embed → store in pgvector → retrieve relevant context at query time → stream LLM answer via Blazor.

**Tech stack:**
| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| UI | Blazor Server (streaming) |
| LLM | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI (`gpt-4o-mini`) |
| Embeddings | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` |
| Vector store | pgvector (Postgres) via VectorData abstractions |
| PDF parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| ORM | EF Core + Npgsql |

**6 work items:**
1. **Project setup** — packages, DI, config
2. **Data model** — EF entities for chat history + pgvector for document chunks
3. **PDF ingestion** — scan `Docs/`, extract text, chunk, embed, upsert
4. **RAG chat service** — embed query → vector search → build prompt → stream answer
5. **Blazor UI** — sidebar sessions, chat area, streaming display
6. **Guardrails** — secrets management, temperature 0, retry logic, token logging

No Agent Framework needed — this is a standard RAG pattern (single LLM call per question). Say **"start"** when you're ready and I'll implement it.
